currentPlan.title: GTA Style Game Project
currentPlan.summary: SCALE & GRID STRATEGY: Utilizing the provided humanoid scale of 1 Character Unit (H) = 5 studs, we will apply size ratios for objects such as cars (3H long), road lanes (2.4H wide), and stories (2.4H tall) to create a cohesive and realistic game environment. The snap formula will ensure accurate placement of objects on the ground level, calculated as Ground_Y = Baseplate.Position.Y + (Baseplate.Size.Y / 2), and then applying Object.Position.Y = Ground_Y + (Object.Size.Y / 2) for each object.

steps.description: SITE SURVEY: Identify existing objects and open spaces in the Workspace. Calculate the size of the baseplate and determine the ground level using the formula Ground_Y = Baseplate.Position.Y + (Baseplate.Size.Y / 2). Assuming a baseplate size of 100x100 studs, the ground level would be at Y = 50. PREDICT SIZES: Calculate the size of a car (3H long) which translates to 3 * 5 = 15 studs in length. For a road lane (2.4H wide), the width would be 2.4 * 5 = 12 studs. COORDINATE MAP: Place the baseplate at [0, 0, 0] and set the ground level at Y = 50. Place a car (ID: 13999609938) at [0, 50 + (15 / 2), 0] = [0, 57.5, 0] and a road lane (using the Main Highway ID: 106470926724188) at [0, 50, 0] with a width of 12 studs. This will serve as the starting point for building the game environment.
